title: Parent Phase B6 — Auth-Mode Tier System
execution_plan_ref: docs/platform/EXECUTION_PLAN.md - M365 CPS Track B P6

principles: Tool-first determinism (capability surface must equal advertised surface)
Operator observability (the system must explain its own state)
Plan-first execution with strict per-phase gates
anti_alignment: Mutation fence on actual write actions stays on
Risk-tier classification per agent stays unchanged
Two-phase confirmation gate for high-risk mutations stays on

user_stated_requirements: Introduce read-only / standard / admin tiers at m365_auth_start. Token store records tier; registry actions declare min_tier; admit() enforces.
intent_capture.intent_verification: Tasks T1..T8 together satisfy this parent phase's objective.

objective: Introduce read-only / standard / admin tiers at m365_auth_start. Token store records tier; registry actions declare min_tier; admit() enforces.
current_state: Parent phase scope draft; no implementation yet.
target_state: All child tasks complete; GATE green; commit + push to feat/m365-cps-trkB.

in_scope: Update token store to record tier
Update registry actions to declare min_tier
file_denylist: Any file not in allowlist of the active child-phase task

requirements.description: Carry-through of master R5; this parent phase contributes scoped behavior.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Per-task exit criteria; parent GATE passes.

requirements.description: Carry-through of master R8; this parent phase contributes scoped behavior.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: Per-task exit criteria; parent GATE passes.

tasks.name: Define scope tier sets
tasks.description: Document the three tiers and their Graph scope sets: read-only={User.Read, Mail.Read, Calendars.Read, Sites.Read.All, Files.Read.All, Team.ReadBasic.All}; standard=read-only + write scopes for Mail/Calendar/Files; admin=standard + Directory.ReadWrite.All, RoleManagement.ReadWrite.Directory.

tasks.name: Update m365_auth_start to accept tier
tasks.description: Add tier parameter to m365_auth_start endpoint and OAuth flow. Default tier=read-only.

tasks.name: Update token store to record tier
tasks.description: Token store schema gains tier field. Migration: existing tokens default to read-only.

tasks.name: Update registry actions to declare min_tier
tasks.description: Each ActionSpec gains min_tier field (read-only by default; admin reads use admin).
deliverables: src/m365_runtime/graph/registry.py with min_tier on each spec
tasks.validation_command: cd $M365_ROOT && .venv/bin/python -c "from m365_runtime.graph.registry import get_action; assert get_action('graph.users.list').min_tier == 'read-only'"

tasks.name: Update admit() to enforce tier
tasks.description: admit() rejects with 'tier_insufficient' if session.tier < spec.min_tier.

tasks.name: Unit tests both repos
tasks.validation_command: cd $M365_ROOT && .venv/bin/python -m pytest -k tier -x && cd $UCP_ROOT && .venv/bin/python -m pytest -k tier -x

tasks.name: Live tenant smoke (each tier)
tasks.description: Auth at each tier; verify scope set returned by Graph matches expected.

tasks.name: Pre-commit + commit
tasks.validation_command: cd $M365_ROOT && pre-commit run --all-files && make test && git log --oneline -1 | grep 'plan:m365-cps-trkB-p6'

checks.name: Parent phase B6 integration
checks.description: All child tasks T1..T8 green; integration test pass

no_go_triggers: Any child task fails its validation_command

agent_constraints: Do not touch files outside Section 5 file allowlist.
Do not skip or reorder tasks (Section 7 strict ordering).
Do not modify thresholds or invariants to make gates green without owner approval.
Do not execute until plan status is approved.
Reference plan:{plan-id}:{task-id} in all action log entries and commits.
Do not add tasks, gates, or deliverables not in this plan without owner approval.
Call validate_action MCP tool before any write operation (UCP server).
Each child phase commits locally; no push until parent-phase GATE is green.
Each parent-phase GATE pushes to feat/m365-cps-trk{X} branch only.
Each track GATE merges to development (UCP) and main (M365) only after live tenant smoke green.
